_readme: The .uitkx scanner contract corpus (family leg 3). familyCore sections are mirrored
byte-identical from the Unreal leg (UETKX->UITKX substitution only) and hashed by
scripts/corpus-hash.mjs into Plans~/family-corpus.hash (must match the frozen family hash).
Every `at`/`expect` value is a Unicode CODE POINT index. skipNoncodeMarkup / findMatchingMarkup
= the family markup lexis; fileScan = the family import/export/mixed grammar.
perLeg sections (skipNoncode/findMatching = C# lexis, fileScanLeg = Unity useX casing) are
engine-specific, NOT hashed, and are populated as the Unity scanner replay lands (see TD).

_doc: Corpus mirroring partition (A4 / TD-009). familyCore sections are BYTE-IDENTICAL
family-wide after the code-prefix substitution (UITKX|GUITKX|UITKX -> TKX) and are
hashed by scripts/corpus-hash.mjs into plans/family-corpus.hash; sibling repos adopt
the same script + hash file (release-time hash-match resolves TD-009). perLeg sections
are engine-specific lexis (C++/GDScript/C#) and are NOT part of the family hash.
nonBmp cases are routed by their per-case `section` field into whichever tier owns it.

skipNoncodeMarkup.name: hash is literal text in markup

findMatchingMarkup.name: component body: prelude comment with paren does not desync
if header is code then body braces
markup text hash inside return window line classified prelude
code island bracket inherits code lexis

nonBmp.name: emoji in string counts as ONE code point (skipNoncode)

_fileScanDoc: fileScan cases run FUetkxFileScan::Scan (C++) / scanFile (TS) over a whole .uetkx source and
assert the parsed shape: imports (names+specifier), components/hooks/modules (name+exported),
the source `order`, and the exact set of diagnostic CODES. `basename` defaults to "" (so the
component/file-name nudge 0103 never fires). `noError` asserts hasError()==false (LINT-only).
fileScan = family-core import/export/mixed grammar (byte-identical family-wide); fileScanLeg =
per-leg hook casing (Unreal/Unity PascalCase Use*, Godot snake_case use_*).

fileScan.name: import one name
import multiple names, ~/ root specifier
import parent-relative specifier
import non-BMP specifier keeps code points intact
multiple imports, mixed specifier forms
export module
fileScan.input: export module Styles { inline const float Pad = 4.0f; }

fileScan.name: mixed component + module preserves source order
duplicate import is UITKX2303
import after a declaration is UITKX2309
two components is the one-per-file convention warn UITKX2105 (not an error)
